DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF MEDICANN, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION TO CONTINUE ARBITRATION DEADLINE AND TO REOPEN DISCOVERY IS DENIED. AS THE MOTION SEEKS TO CONTINUE THE ARBITRATION DEADLINE, THAT MOTION MUST BE MADE IN THE PRE-TRIAL DEPARTMENT. UNLIKE THE DISCOVERY CUT-OFF WHICH OCCURS 15 DAYS PRIOR TO THE DATE FIRST SET FOR TRIAL, DISCOVERY CUT-OFF FOR ARBITRATION DOES NOT OCCUR, EXCEPT WHEN THE ARBITRATION ACTUALLY TAKES PLACE. NO APPEARANCES; TENTATIVE ADOPTED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

PLAINTIFF MEDICANN, INCORPORATED'S Amended MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Classify Action As Unlimited IS GRANTED; PLAINTIFF MAY FILE ITS PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Continue Arbitration Deadline And To Reopen Discovery
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MEDICANN, INC.'s Amended Motion To Continue Arbitration Deadline And To Reopen Discovery is denied. As the motion seeks to continue the arbitration deadline, that motion must be made in the Pre-Trial Department. Unlike the discovery cut-off which occurs 15 days prior to the date first set for trial, discovery cut-off for arbitration does not occur, except when the arbitration actually takes place. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
